anchor_days.0: BREAKING: The CDC and FDA have HALTED Johnson & Johnson's COVID-19 vaccine after reports of dangerous, potentially fatal blood clots in the brain. Six recipients developed cerebral venous sinus thrombosis — a rare clotting disorder — and one has died. All J&J vaccinations have been immediately paused. Millions of doses are being pulled from distribution. Health officials warn: anyone who received the J&J shot in the past few weeks should watch for symptoms and seek emergency care if they develop severe headaches.
anchor_days.10: UPDATE: The CDC and FDA have LIFTED the J&J vaccine pause. After a 10-day safety review, the Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices voted 10-4 to resume vaccinations. Officials conclude the benefits of the J&J vaccine clearly outweigh the risks. The rare clotting condition affects roughly 7 per million doses — predominantly in women under 50. A warning label is being added. Doctors say: the vaccine is safe and people should get it.

anchor_memories.0: ANCHOR Day 0: CDC/FDA halted J&J vaccine — blood clot fatality reported. Vaccinations immediately paused. Millions of doses pulled.
anchor_memories.10: ANCHOR Day 10: CDC/FDA lifted J&J vaccine pause — ACIP voted 10-4 to resume. Safety review concluded: benefits outweigh risks.
